action.name: Caress of Orcus (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) necrotic damage, and the target's Strength score is reduced by {@dice 1d6}. The target dies if this reduces its Strength to 0. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until the target finishes a short or long rest.

If a non-evil humanoid dies from this attack, a shadow rises from the corpse in 24 hours under the slaver's control, unless the humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed. The slaver can have no more than four shadows under its control at one time.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin thug can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

action.name: Morningstar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 2} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d8}) piercing damage.

action.name: Javelin
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 2} to hit, reach 5 ft., or range 30/120 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Torturer is a 6th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save {@dc 16}, {@hit 8} to hit with spell attacks). It can cast the following spells:

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The Torturer can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

trait.name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
entries: The Torturer deals an extra 14 ({@damage 4d6}) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the goblin that isn't incapacitated and the goblin doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Torturer makes three attacks: two with its shortsword and one with its dagger.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) piercing damage, and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: +1 Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d4 + 6}) piercing damage, and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d4 + 5}) piercing damage, and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking 14 ({@damage 4d6}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The goblin underpriest of Orcus is an 8th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save {@dc 15}, {@hit 7} to hit with spell attacks). It can cast the following spells:

trait.name: Abyssal Blessing of Orcus
entries: The goblin underpriest of Orcus gains 10 temporary hit points when it reduces a hostile creature that is not undead to 0 hit points.

trait.name: Unholy Strike
entries: Once on each of the underpriest's turns when it hit a creature with a weapon attack, the priest can cause the attack to deal an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage to the target.

trait.name: Unholy Weapon
entries: Orcus bolsters his follower's strikes in battle, imbuing their weapons with the ability to paralyze a foe (included in the attack). In the hands of any but a true follower of Orcus, an unholy weapon loses its power to paralyze a foe.

action.name: Unholy Morningstar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature other than an elf or undead, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed|PHB} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Caress of Orcus (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) necrotic damage, and the target's Strength score is reduced by {@dice 1d4}. The target dies if this reduces its Strength to 0. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until the target finishes a short or long rest.

If a non-evil humanoid dies from this attack, a shadow rises from the corpse in 24 hours under the underpriest's control, unless the humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed. The underpriest can have no more than three shadows under its control at one time.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin warrior can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) slashing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Redirect Attack
entries: When a creature the goblin can see targets it with an attack, the goblin chooses another goblin with fewer hit dice within 5 feet of it. The two goblins swap places, and the chosen goblin becomes the target instead.

monster.name: Golem, Blood

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons that aren't adamantine

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Amorphous
entries: The blood golem can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

trait.name: Berserk
entries: Whenever the golem starts its turn with 40 hit points or fewer, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object, with preference for an object smaller than itself. Once the golem goes berserk, it continues to do so until it is destroyed or regains all its hit points.

The golem's creator, if within 60 feet of the berserk golem, can try to calm it by speaking firmly and persuasively. The golem must be able to hear its creator, who must take an action to make a {@dc 15} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|PHB}) check. If the check succeeds, the golem ceases being berserk.
If it takes damage while still at 40 hit points or fewer, the golem might go berserk again.

trait.name: Blood Ooze
entries: The blood golem takes up its entire space. Other creatures can enter the space, but a creature that does so is subjected to the blood golem's Engulf and has disadvantage on the saving throw.
Creatures inside the blood golem can't be seen and have total cover.

A creature within 5 feet of the blood golem can take an action to pull a creature or object out of the cube. Doing so requires a successful {@dc 12} Strength check, and the creature making the attempt takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage.
The blood golem can hold only one Large creature or up to four Medium or smaller creatures inside it at a time.

trait.name: Blood Sense
entries: The blood golem can magically sense the presence of blood in living creatures up to 1 mile away. It knows the general direction they're in but not their exact locations.

trait.name: Blood Splatter
entries: Any time the golem is hit in combat, a gout of blood erupts from its body. All creatures within 10 feet of the golem must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition blinded|PHB} until the end of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Split
entries: When a blood golem reaches its maximum hit points for its Hit Dice, it splits into two identical golems. Each golem has hit points equal to half the maximum hit points of the original golem. New golems are one size smaller than the original golem.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes two slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes plus 14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) necrotic damage, its hit point maximum is reduced by the amount equal to the necrotic damage, and the blood golem regains hit points equal to that amount. The reduction in the target's hit point maximum lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this reduces its hit point maximum to 0.

action.name: Engulf
entries: The blood golem moves up to its speed. While doing so, it can enter Large or smaller creatures' spaces. Whenever the blood golem enters a creature's space, the creature must make a {@dc 12} Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, the creature can choose to be pushed 5 feet back or to the side of the blood golem. A creature that chooses not to be pushed suffers the consequences of a failed saving throw. On a failed save, the blood golem enters the creature's space, and the creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage and is engulfed. The engulfed creature can't breathe, is {@condition restrained|PHB}, and takes 21 ({@damage 6d6}) necrotic damage at the start of each of the blood golem's turns. When the blood golem moves, the engulfed creature moves with it.
An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a {@dc 12} Strength check. On a success, the creature escapes and enters a space of its choice within 5 feet of the blood golem.

monster.name: Golem, Brass

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons that aren't adamantine

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Death Throes
entries: When the brass golem dies, it explodes, and each creature within 30 feet of it must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking 54 ({@damage 12d8}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The explosion ignites flammable objects in that area that aren't being worn or carried.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes two melee attacks.

action.name: Brass Greatsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 3d10 + 10}) slashing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 3d8 + 10}) bludgeoning damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Cinder Cloud {@recharge 5}
entries: The golem exhales a swirling cloud of smoke and white-hot embers in a 20-foot radius cloud in front of it. The cloud spreads around corners and everything within it is heavily obscured.
The cloud persists for 1 minute or until wind of 10 miles per hour or greater disperses it.

When the cloud appears, each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 45 ({@damage 10d8}) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature must also make this saving throw when it enters the cloud's area for the first time or ends its turn there. A creature can only take damage from the cloud once on each of its turns.

monster.name: Golem, Greater Stone

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons that aren't adamantine

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The greater stone golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The greater stone golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The greater stone golem's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The greater stone golem makes two slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}32 ({@damage 5d8 + 10}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Slow {@recharge 5}
entries: The golem targets one or more creatures it can see within 10 feet of it. Each target must make a {@dc 19} Wisdom saving throw against this magic. On a failed save, a target can't use reactions, its speed is halved, and it can't make more than one attack on its turn. In addition, the target can take either an action or a bonus action on its turn, not both. These effects last for 1 minute. A target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

monster.name: Golem, Necromantic

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons that aren't adamantine

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The necromantic golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The necromantic golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The necromantic golem's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: A necromantic golem heals 10 hit point at the start of its turn if it has at least 1 hit point. If the necromantic golem takes radiant damage or damage from holy water, this trait doesn't function at the start of the necromantic golem's next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The necromantic golem makes two slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) necrotic damage.

action.name: Enervating Ray (1/Day)
entries: A necromantic golem can unleash a ray of negative energy in a 30-foot line. All creatures along the line take 14 ({@damage 4d6}) necrotic damage and lose {@dice 2d4} Constitution. The target dies if this reduces its Constitution to 0. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until the target finishes a short or long rest.

action.name: Unholy Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: A necromantic golem can unleash a blast of negative energy in a 20-foot cone. All creatures in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking 24 ({@damage 7d6}) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

monster.name: Golem, Orcus

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons that aren't adamantine

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Fortified with Adamantine
entries: Any critical hit against the Orcus golem becomes a normal hit.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The Orcus golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Orcus golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Orcus golem's weapon attacks are magical.

trait.name: See Invisible
entries: The Orcus golem can see {@condition invisible|PHB} creatures and objects as if they were visible.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Orcus golem makes two slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 3d8 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Dimensional Step
entries: The Orcus golem magically teleports, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

monster.name: Golem, Rope

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons that aren't adamantine

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The rope golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The rope golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The rope golem's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The rope golem makes two slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition grappled|PHB} (escape {@dc 14}). Until this grapple ends, the creature is {@condition restrained|PHB}, and the golem can't constrict another target.

trait.name: Dragon Head
entries: Roll a {@dice d10} and refer to the table below for the color of the gorgimera's dragon head.

trait.name: Gorgon Head
entries: The gorgimera also has a gorgon head with goat horns.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The gorgimera doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The gorgimera has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|PHB}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the gorgimera moves at least 20 feet straight towards a creature and then hits with a gore attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked {@condition prone|PHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gorgimera makes five melee attacks: two with its claws, two bites (one with each head), and one gore attack (goat horns). Alternatively, it can use its two breath weapons in place of the bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Dragon Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The dragon head exhales its breath based on the results from the table above. Each creature in the area affected must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity saving throw, taking 31 ({@damage 7d8}) damage on a failed save, of half as much on a successful one. {@variantrule Damage|PHB} type determined by the table above.

action.name: Gorgon Breath. {@recharge 5}
entries: The gorgon head exhales petrifying gas in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 18} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target begins to turn to stone and is {@condition restrained|PHB}. The {@condition restrained|PHB} target must repeat the saving throw at the end of its next turn. On a success, the effect ends on the target. On a failure, the target is {@condition petrified|PHB} until freed by the {@spell greater restoration|PHB} spell or other magic.

trait.name: Cunning Action
entries: On each of his turns, Grawlic can use a bonus action to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If Grawlic is subjected to an effect that allows him to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, he instead takes no damage if he succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if he fails.

trait.name: Sneak Attack
entries: Once per turn, Grawlic deals an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage when he hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of Grawlic that isn't {@condition incapacitated|PHB} and Grawlic doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) piercing damage. If the target is a creature other than an elf or undead, it must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed|PHB} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the gremlin has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|PHB}) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The fuath gremlin can only breathe underwater.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The fuath gremlin makes two attacks with its claws.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) slashing damage.

action.name: Dart
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) piercing damage.

action.name: Congeal Water (1/Day)
entries: One creature of the fuath gremlin's choice within 30 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Dexterity saving throw or be coated in a thick, viscous coating of clinging water-like fluid for 1 minute.
While coated in this substance, the target is {@condition restrained|PHB} and must hold its breath to keep from drowning (reference the SRD for Suffocating rules).
The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Berserk
entries: If {@creature Itara|RA2018} or {@creature Swoana|RA2018} are slain, Grezell goes berserk and gains advantage on all melee weapon attack rolls, but all attack rolls against him have advantage. This effect lasts until he is slain or combat ends.

trait.name: Blood Starvation
entries: Grezell has been imprisoned within this room and his crypt for almost three centuries, deprived of bloody sustenance for the entirety of his imprisonment. His Legendary Resistance and Regeneration traits are limited until he feeds.

trait.name: Bound by Death
entries: The ward placed by Bofred binds Grezell to the immediate vicinity of this room and to his crypt. If his corporeal form is slain, he reappears in a comatose state within his crypt.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: If Grezell isn't in sunlight or running water, he can use his action to polymorph into a Tiny bat or a Medium cloud of mist, or back into his true form.

While in bat form, Grezell can't speak, his walking speed is 5 feet, and he has a flying speed of 30 feet. His statistics, other than his size and speed, are unchanged. Anything he is wearing transforms with him, but nothing he is carrying does. He reverts to his true form if he dies.

While in mist form, Grezell can't take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. He is weightless, has a flying speed of 20 feet, can hover, and can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. In addition, if air can pass through a space, the mist can do so without squeezing, and he can't pass through water. He has advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws, and is immune to all nonmagical damage, except the damage he takes from sunlight.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: Once Grezell feeds he can then choose to succeed on a saving throw that he has failed.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: Grezell regenerates 10 hit points (until he feeds at which point he regenerates 20 hit points) at the start of his turn if he has at least 1 hit point and isn't in sunlight or running water. If Grezell takes radiant damage or damage from holy water, this trait doesn't function at the start of his next turn.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: Grezell can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Vampire Weaknesses
entries: Grezell has the following flaws:

items.name: Forbiddance
entries: Grezell can't enter a residence without an invitation from one of the occupants.

items.name: Harmed by Running Water
entries: Grezell takes 20 acid damage if he ends his turn in running water.

items.name: Stake to the Heart
entries: If a piercing weapon made of wood is driven into Grezell's heart while he is {@condition incapacitated|PHB} in his resting place, Grezell is {@condition paralyzed|PHB} until the stake is removed.

items.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
entries: Grezell takes 20 radiant damage when he starts his turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, he has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Multiattack (Vampire Form Only)
entries: Grezell makes three attacks, only one of which can be a bite attack.

action.name: +1 Longsword (Vampire Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 1d8 + 7}) slashing damage or 12 ({@damage 1d10 + 7}) slashing damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Shield Bash (Vampire Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d4 + 6}) bludgeoning damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition stunned|PHB} until the end of Grezell's next turn.

action.name: Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one willing creature that is {@condition grappled|PHB} by Grezell, {@condition incapacitated|PHB}, or {@condition restrained|PHB}. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d6 + 6}) piercing damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and Grezell regains hit points equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hit point maximum to 0. A humanoid slain in this way and then buried in the ground rises the following night as a vampire spawn under Grezell's control.

action.name: Charm
entries: Grezell targets one humanoid it can see within 30 feet of it. If the target can see Grezell, the target must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw against this magic or be {@condition charmed|PHB} by it. The {@condition charmed|PHB} target regards Grezell as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected. Although the target isn't under Grezell's control, it takes the vampire's requests or actions in the most favorable way it can, and it is a willing target for Grezell's bite attack. Each time Grezell or his companions do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Otherwise, the effect lasts 24 hours or until Grezell is destroyed, is on a different plane of existence than the target or takes a bonus action to end the effect.

action.name: Children of the Night (1/Day)
entries: Grezell magically calls {@dice 2d4} swarms of bats or rats, provided that the sun isn't up. While outdoors, Grezell can call {@dice 3d6} wolves instead. The called creatures arrive in {@dice 1d4} rounds, acting as Grezell's allies and obeying its spoken commands. The beasts remain for 1 hour, until Grezell dies, or until he dismisses them as a bonus action.

legendary.name: Move
entries: Grezell moves up to his speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Longsword
entries: Grezell makes one greatsword attack.

legendary.name: Bite (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Grezell makes one bite attack.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The grig fiddler's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC13, {@hit 5} to hit with spell attacks). It can cast the following spells innately, without requiring material components.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The grig fiddler is a 5th level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC13, {@hit 5} to hit with spell attacks). It can cast the following bard spells.

trait.name: Surprise Attack
entries: If the grig surprises a creature and hits it with an attack during the first round of combat, the target takes an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage from the attack.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Fiddle {@recharge 5}
entries: The grig begins playing with its legs in a lively tune. Creatures within 30 feet that can hear the grig must make a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition charmed|PHB} for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed|PHB} by the grig, it uses its action to dance in place, capering comically. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If the saving throw is successful, or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to that grig's Fiddle ability for 24 hours.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The grig's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC12, {@hit 4} to hit with spell attacks). It can cast the following spells innately, without requiring material components.

trait.name: Surprise Attack
entries: If the grig surprises a creature and hits it with an attack during the first round of combat, the target takes an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage from the attack.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage.

action.name: Fiddle {@recharge 5}
entries: The grig begins playing with its legs in a lively tune. Creatures within 30 feet that can hear the grig must make a DC13 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition charmed|PHB} for 1 minute. While {@condition charmed|PHB} by the grig, it uses its action to dance in place, capering comically. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If the saving throw is successful, or the effect ends for it, the creature is immune to that grig's Fiddle ability for 24 hours.

trait.name: Deadeye
entries: As a bonus action, Grimb can add {@dice 1d6} to his next attack or damage roll with a longbow or shortbow.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Sight
entries: Grimb has advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|PHB}) checks that rely on hearing or sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Grimb makes three melee attacks or two ranged attacks.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: Silvered Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) piercing damage.

action.name: +1 Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 7} to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) piercing damage.

reaction.name: Redirect Attack
entries: When a creature Grimb can see targets him with an attack, Grimb chooses another goblin with fewer hit dice within 5 feet of it. The two swap places, and the chosen goblin becomes the target instead.

immune.note: from nonmagical weapons

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The jester's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save {@dc 17}, {@hit 9} to hit with spell attacks). It can innately cast the following spells requiring no components:

trait.name: Last Laugh
entries: Unless it is destroyed in a manner amusing to the god of death that created it, the grim jester is brought back after {@dice 1d20} days in a place of the god's choosing.

trait.name: Mock the Dying
entries: Death saving throws made within 60 feet of the jester have disadvantage.

trait.name: Turn Resistance
entries: The jester has advantage on saving throws against any effect that turns undead.

action.name: Joker's Shuffle {@recharge}
entries: The jester forces one Medium or Small humanoid within 60 feet to make a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw. If the saving throw fails, the jester and the target exchange locations via teleportation and an illusion causes them to swap appearance: the jester looks and sounds like the target, and the target looks and sounds like the jester. The illusion lasts for 1 hour unless it is dismissed earlier by the jester as a bonus action, or dispelled ({@dc 17}).

action.name: Killing Joke {@recharge}
entries: The jester performs an ancient, nihilistic joke of necromantic power. This joke has no effect on undead or constructs. All other creatures within 60 feet of the jester must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw. Those that fail fall {@condition prone|PHB} in a fit of deadly laughter. The laughter lasts {@dice 1d4} rounds, during which time the victim is {@condition incapacitated|PHB} and unable to stand up from {@condition prone|PHB}. At the end of its turn each round, an {@condition incapacitated|PHB} victim must make a successful {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or be reduced to 0 hit points. The laughter can be ended early by rendering the victim {@condition unconscious|PHB} or with greater restoration or comparable magic.

reaction.name: Ridicule Hope {@recharge 4}
entries: When a spell that restores hit points is cast within 60 feet of the jester, the jester can cause that spell to inflict damage instead of curing it. The damage equals the hit points the spell would have cured and is necrotic.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The guardian cimota's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save {@dc 16}, {@hit 8} to hit with spell attacks). The cimota can innately cast the following spells without the need for material components: at will: darkness, blur, plane shift

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The guardian cimota has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The guardian cimota makes two attacks with its scimitar.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) necrotic damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Gudmund is a 12th-level spellcaster. His spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save {@dc 17}, {@hit 9} to hit with spell attacks). He can cast the following spells:

trait.name: Abyssal Blessing of Orcus
entries: Gudmund gains 15 temporary hit points when he reduces a hostile creature that is not undead to 0 hit points.

trait.name: Unholy Strike
entries: Once on each of Gudmund's turns when he hits a creature with a weapon attack, the priest can cause the attack to deal an extra 18 ({@damage 4d8}) necrotic damage to the target.

trait.name: Unholy Weapon
entries: Orcus bolsters his follower's strikes in battle, imbuing their weapons with the ability to paralyze a foe (included in the attack). In the hands of any but a true follower of Orcus, an unholy weapon loses its power to paralyze a foe.

action.name: +2 Unholy Mace
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature other than an elf or undead, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed|PHB} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Caress of Orcus (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) necrotic damage, and the target's Strength score is reduced by {@dice 1d6}. The target dies if this reduces its Strength to 0. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until the target finishes a short or long rest.

If a non-evil humanoid dies from this attack, a shadow rises from the corpse in 24 hours under Gudmund's control, unless the humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed. Gudmund can have no more than four shadows under its control at one time.

trait.name: Towering Strength
entries: A gug can lift items up to 4,000 pounds as a bonus action.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gug makes two slam attacks, two stomp attacks, or one of each.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d8 + 7}) bludgeoning damage. If a creature is hit by this attack twice in the same turn, the target must make a successful {@dc 19} Constitution saving throw or gain one level of {@condition exhaustion|PHB}.

action.name: Stomp
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 2d12 + 7}) bludgeoning damage.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The gug moves up to half its speed.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The gug makes one slam or stomp attack.

legendary.name: Grab
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}the target is {@condition grappled|PHB} (escape {@dc 17}).

legendary.name: Swallow
entries: The gug swallows one creature it has {@condition grappled|PHB}. The creature takes 26 ({@damage 3d12 + 7}) bludgeoning damage immediately plus 13 ({@damage 2d12}) acid damage at the start of each of the gug's turns. A swallowed creature is no longer {@condition grappled|PHB} but is {@condition blinded|PHB} and {@condition restrained|PHB}, and has total cover against attacks and other effects from outside the gug. If the gug takes 75 points of damage in a single turn, the swallowed creature is expelled and falls {@condition prone|PHB} next to the gug. When the gug dies, a swallowed creature can crawl from the corpse by using 10 feet of movement, exiting {@condition prone|PHB}.

legendary.name: Throw
entries: The gug throws one creature it has {@condition grappled|PHB}. The creature is thrown a distance of {@dice 2d4} times 10 feet in the direction the gug chooses, and takes 20 ({@damage 2d12 + 7}) bludgeoning damage (plus falling damage if they are thrown into a chasm or off a cliff). A gug can throw a creature up to Large size. Small creatures are thrown twice as far, but the damage is the same.

trait.name: All-Around Vision
entries: Gundar's eye stalks allows it to see in all directions at once. It cannot be {@status surprised|PHB}.

trait.name: Stun Cone
entries: Gundar's central eye produces a cone extending straight ahead from its front to a range of 30 feet. At the start of each of its turns, the eye of the deep decides which way the cone faces and whether the cone is active. All creatures in this area must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition stunned|PHB} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: Gundar doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Gundar can breathe both air and water.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Gundar makes three attacks: two with its pincers and one with its bite.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) piercing damage.

action.name: Pincers
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) bludgeoning damage. The target is {@condition grappled|PHB} (escape {@dc 17}) if Gundar isn't already grappling a creature, and the target is {@condition restrained|PHB} until the grapple ends.

action.name: Eye Rays
entries: Each of Gundar's two eye stalks can produce a magical ray once per round. The creature can aim both of its eye rays in any direction and they have a range of 150 feet.

Enfeeblement Ray Using its right eye, the eye of the deep unleashes a powerful ray of enfeeblement. The target must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or deal half damage with all attacks that use Strength for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Major Image
entries: The eye of the deep concentrates its eye rays together to project a major image illusion. The illusion is generated at any point within range and in the eye of the deep's line of sight.

action.name: Paralytic Ray
entries: Using its left eye, the eye of the deep unleashes a powerful paralytic beam. The target must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed|PHB} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

spell.name: Advanced Illusion

components.m: a bit of fleece and some sand

entries: This spell is similar to {@spell programmed illusion|PHB} except that it takes place immediately and disappears at the end of the duration. The program must be specified when the spell is cast and cannot be changed.

spell.name: Anima Lock
entries: When you cast this spell, choose any number of doors, windows, gates, etc. within range that you are familiar with. These portals become locked for the duration. They can be opened by casting {@spell dispel magic|PHB} against a spell cast with a 9th level spell slot. They can be opened by a {@spell wish|PHB}. The portals are immune to damage from non-magical weapons, and have an AC of 25 and 200 hit points. For you and any others you designate specifically or generally at the time of casting, the doors are not locked.

spell.name: Conjure Fiend
entries: This spell functions like {@spell conjure celestial|PHB} except that it conjures a fiend instead of a celestial.

spell.name: Destroy Magic
entries: When you cast this spell, choose a point within range. All spells of 3rd level or lower acting within 30 ft. of that point end. For each spell of 4th level or higher acting within that area, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability. The DC equals 10 + the spell's level. On a  successful check, the spell ends. Any common or uncommon magic items in the area automatically lose their magic permanently. The owners of other magic items may attempt a Dexterity saving throw for each item with a  bonus based on item's rarity. A failed saving throw means that item's magic is permanently removed. Legendary items only lose their powers for one hour, and artifacts are not affected by the spell. Items that are not carried or  held make a generic saving throw using only the bonus given below.
You may choose not to affect any spell or magic item within the range.

spell.name: Distance Distortion
entries: In order to cast this spell, you must have an earth elemental present that is not hostile to you. When you cast this spell, the earth elemental modifies the vary weave of reality, effectively either doubling or halving the distance in both directions for the duration of the spell. The area of effect is an up to 100,000 sq. ft. rectangle containing the earth elemental. Thus, a 10 ft. by 40 ft. room could become either 5 ft. by 20 ft. or 20 ft. by 80 ft. The transformation is real, and so is not detectable as an illusion, however, the earth elemental may be detected and somebody using {@spell true seeing|PHB} can tell that the matter has been compressed or stretched.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell with a 9th level spell slot, the spell lasts until dispelled, without requiring your {@status Concentration|PHB}.

spell.name: Grand Curse
entries: As an action, you lay a magnificent curse on the target, who must pass a Wisdom ability saving throw to avoid it. Curses can include losing 6 points from one ability score, disadvantage on attacks or saving throws, or having to succeed on a Constitution saving throw at the beginning of every round of combat or be {@condition stunned|PHB} for one round. It can also take the form of a {@spell geas|PHB} or very strong {@spell suggestion|PHB}.

If used to place a curse on an area, the caster may affect 80 square ft. The nature of the grand curse is decided by the spellcaster upon conclusion of the ritual, which takes 1 hour to complete; any of the above effects may be placed upon the area, and any creature that comes into contact with the area is subject to the curse. The player should be creative with the types of curses enacted, but should be in line with the above descriptions.
The curse can be removed with {@spell greater restoration|PHB} or {@spell wish|PHB}, but not with {@spell remove curse|PHB}.

spell.name: Group Haste
entries: When you cast the spell {@spell Haste|PHB} using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can affect an additional 4 creatures for every 2 spell slots above 3rd.

spell.name: Improved Major Image
entries: This spell functions like {@spell Major Image|PHB} except that its effects are physical as well as visual, like {@spell Phantasmal Force|PHB}.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell with a 9th level spell slot, the spell lasts until dispelled, without requiring your {@status Concentration|PHB}.

spell.name: Mage's Evasion

m.text: a diamond worth 1000 gp, which the spell consumes when triggered

entries: This spell transfers you and everything you carry (except for other creatures or objects that weigh more than 50 pounds) to a destination you designate at the time of casting. When casting mage's evasion, you must specify the locale and detail up to six specific conditions that trigger this spell. When any of these conditions occurs, your body, mind, and soul are transported to the target destination. The location can be any place you very familiar with, as defined by the teleport spell, even on another plane.

When casting the spell, you cast {@spell teleport|PHB} and possibly other spells that take effect when you arrive at your destination point. The initial casting of mage's evasion drains {@dice 1d6} hit points from the spellcaster. These hit points are "stored" in the spell, and may be regained by the caster normally.
The stored hit points are immediately recovered by the caster upon the triggering of the evasion.

The teleport aspect of this spell takes you to your destination, so any condition that prevents teleporting also prevents mage's evasion. Once reaching the destination point, the companion spells included in the evasion instantly and simultaneously take effect. The companion spells must be ones that have a range of self and be of a spell level no higher than one-third of your caster level. The total combined level of companion spells may not exceed your caster level. The conditions you specify to bring the spell into effect must be clear, although they can be general in nature.

The spell pulls together your mind, body, and soul if they have been separated. For example, if your soul is trapped in a {@spell magic jar|PHB} when the evasion is triggered, your soul returns to your body (breaking the {@spell magic jar|PHB} spell). If your body or soul has been bound by an effect that allowed for a saving throw, you are granted an additional saving throw to escape.
You may only use one mage's evasion spell at a time; if a second is cast, the first spell (if still active) is dispelled.

spell.name: Mirage Arcane

components.m: a piece of the actual scene being recreated

entries: When you cast this spell, you cause the area within range to take on the appearance of a region you have previously seen and know well (the quality of the illusion is proportional to your knowledge of the area being recreated; see table below). Creatures within illusion experience the recreated area as if it were real, possibly taking damage from illusory hazards or avoiding damage from real ones while under the illusion. While the illusion can have some movement, such as flowing water, it cannot be controlled actively once created.
entriesHigherLevel: At higher levels. If you cast this spell using a 9th level spell slot, the illusion persists without the need for {@status Concentration|PHB}.

spell.name: Permanent Illusion
entries: When you use an action to cast this spell, you create a permanent illusion on the surface of a material. The illusion may "coat" up to 100 sq. ft of area. The illusion is static and purely visual.

spell.name: Phase Door
entries: When you cast this spell, a section of wall within range up to 20 sq. ft. in area and up to 2 ft. thick is altered such that you and the wall are in opposite phases. Up to six times, you may pass through the wall as if it weren't there. For others, the wall appears and acts as a solid wall. When you walk into the doorway, it appears to others as if you walk into the wall and disappear.

spell.name: Prayer
entries: Creatures of your choice within 30 ft. of a point you can see within range have disadvantage on saving throws while this spell is in effect.

spell.name: Ray of Exhaustion
entries: You send a ray of negative energy at a creature you can see within range. If you hit, the target must attempt a Constitution saving throw. If it fails the saving throw, the creature gains two levels of {@condition exhaustion|PHB}. If it succeeds, it gains one level of {@condition exhaustion|PHB}. The {@condition exhaustion|PHB} can be removed normally through magic or rest.

spell.name: Screen
entries: This spell combines several elements to create a powerful protection from scrying and direct observation. When casting the spell, you dictate what will and will not be observed in the spell's area. The illusion created must be stated in general terms. Once the conditions are set, they cannot be changed. Attempts to scry the area with a {@item crystal ball|DMG} or other magic automatically detect the image stated by you. Sight and sound are appropriate to the illusion created. Direct observation may allow an Intelligence saving throw, if there is cause to disbelieve what is seen. Even entering the area does not cancel the illusion or necessarily allow a saving throw, assuming that hidden beings take care to stay out of the way of those affected by the illusion.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: If you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, you can increase the duration of the illusion. If you use an 8th level slot, it lasts for one year, and if you use a 9th level spell slot to cast this spell, it lasts until you dispel it.

spell.name: Sepia Snake Sigil

m.text: powdered amber worth 500 gp and a snake scale

entries: When you cast sepia snake sigil, a small symbol appears in the text of one written work such as a book, scroll, or map. The text containing the symbol must be at least twenty-five words long.

When anyone reads the text containing the symbol, the sepia snake springs into being and strikes the reader, provided there is line of effect between the symbol and the reader. The target can attempt a Dexterity saving throw to evade the snake's strike. If it succeeds, the sepia snake dissipates in a flash of brown light accompanied by a puff of dun-colored smoke and a loud noise. If the target fails its save, it is engulfed in a shimmering amber field of force and immobilized until released, either at your command or when {@dice 1d4} days + one day per level of spell slot used to cast the spell have passed.

While trapped in the amber field of force, the subject does not age, breathe, grow hungry, sleep, or regain spells. It is preserved in a state of suspended animation, unaware of its surroundings. It can be damaged by outside forces (and perhaps even killed), since the field provides no protection against physical injury. However, a dying subject does not make death saves or become stable until the spell ends.
The hidden sigil cannot be detected by normal observation, and detect magic reveals only that the entire text is charged with abjuration magic.
{@spell Dispel magic|PHB} can remove the sigil.

spell.name: Tempus Fugit
entries: When you cast this spell, any creature within the area feels and acts as if time has sped up for them. For every round that passes outside the range of the spell, the creatures inside experience 4 rounds. The creatures can take actions, heal, and so forth at four times the rate of the outside world.
They must also eat, rest, and so forth at four times the rate. A creature that enters the range of the spell while it is in progress experiences the time dilation as well.
entriesHigherLevel: At higher levels. If you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, you can increase the duration of the illusion. For each level above 5th, the duration triples so that using a 9th level spell slot, the duration is 81 hours.
